name: WikiWonder
description:
Render every storage item to Markdown and write it into a chosen target folder.

## The `plugins:` frontmatter convention

Templates opt in to per-target export by adding a top-level `plugins:` block to their `markdown_template` frontmatter, namespaced by plugin id. WikiWonder reads `plugins.wikiwonder`; a Hugo plugin would read `plugins.hugo`; etc. Every plugin sees only its own block.

```yaml
plugins:
  wikiwonder:
    enabled: true                            # optional; default true. false skips the item.
    title: '{{field "audit-control-naam"}}'
    path: '{{field "audit-control-id"}}'    # optional; subpath/filename. May contain `/`.
    tags:
    {{yamlList (fieldRaw "audit-control-tags")}}
```

**Output frontmatter** = the contents of `plugins.wikiwonder` minus the routing keys (`enabled`, `path`). Anything else passes through verbatim.

**No `plugins.wikiwonder` block?** The rendered (PDF-shaped) frontmatter is stripped by default — toggle *Keep frontmatter when no plugin block* in the form to keep it instead.
author: Peter van de Pas
